Detailed Description

Implementation for the SimJob class.

Definition in file SimJob.cpp.

Function Documentation

◆ cgsqr_()

void cgsqr_ ( dcmplx * ca,
dcmplx * cy,
dcmplx * cx,
int * mxn,
int * n,
double * err,
int * mxiter,
int * iter,
float * time )

External conjugate gradient solver (complex version) from cg.f.

Solves a linear system Ax = y iteratively using the conjugate gradient squared (CGS) algorithm for complex-valued matrices.

Parameters
caPointer to the complex system matrix A (flattened).
cyPointer to the complex right-hand side vector y.
cxPointer to the complex solution vector x (input guess, then result).
mxnTotal number of elements in A.
nDimension of the linear system.
errTarget relative error tolerance.
mxiterMaximum number of iterations allowed.
iterOn exit, the number of iterations performed.
timeOn exit, elapsed time in seconds.
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ CleanUpFiles()

int CleanUpFiles ( std::map< const char *, std::ofstream * > files)

Close and deallocate a set of open output files.

Parameters
filesMap of filenames (as C-string keys) to open file stream pointers.
Returns
Always returns 0.

Iterates through a map of file streams, closing and deleting each associated std::ofstream pointer, then clears the container. Each entry's stream is closed and its pointer freed using delete. The input map is then cleared to remove all entries.

cvec ConjVec ( cvec in)

Compute the element-wise complex conjugate of a 3-vector.

Parameters
inInput complex vector.
Returns
Complex vector containing conjugated components of in.

◆ JacobiPrecondition()

int JacobiPrecondition ( blitz::Array< dcmplx, 2 > * inMatrix,
blitz::Array< dcmplx, 1 > * inVector )

Apply Jacobi preconditioning to a complex linear system.

Parameters
inMatrixPointer to the complex system matrix (modified in place).
inVectorPointer to the complex right-hand side vector (modified in place).
Returns
Always returns 0.

Scales each row of the input matrix and corresponding vector element by the inverse of the diagonal entry, effectively normalizing the system's diagonal to unity. For each row i, this routine multiplies both the i-th vector element and the entire row of inMatrix by 1 / A(i,i).

Definition at line 544 of file SimJob.cpp.

◆ zgetrf_()

void zgetrf_ ( int * dim1,
int * dim2,
dcmplx * a,
int * lda,
int * ipiv,
int * info )

Computes the LU factorization of a general M-by-N matrix.

Parameters
dim1Number of rows of the matrix (for zgetrf_).
dim2Number of columns of the matrix (for zgetrf_).
aPointer to the matrix to be factored or solved.
ldaLeading dimension of the matrix.
ipivPointer to the pivot indices.
infoOutput status (0 if successful).

◆ zgetrs_()

void zgetrs_ ( char * TRANS,
int * N,
int * NRHS,
dcmplx * A,
int * LDA,
int * IPIV,
dcmplx * B,
int * LDB,
int * INFO )

Solves a system of linear equations using the LU factorization.

Parameters
TRANSSpecifies the form of the system of equations ('N' for no transpose, etc.).
NOrder of the coefficient matrix.
NRHSNumber of right-hand sides.
APointer to the LU-factored coefficient matrix.
LDALeading dimension of A.
IPIVPointer to the pivot indices from zgetrf_.
BPointer to the right-hand side vector(s).
LDBLeading dimension of B.
INFOOutput status (0 if successful).
